title: OpRecord
description:
`ops/<op_id>.json` — the durable request identity, persisted (`0600`) BEFORE the first network send so
an uncertain write replays the SAME `op_id` (the server returns the byte-identical receipt — no
double-advance, no duplicate commit). It carries the full bound identity of the op — the workspace,
skill, kind, candidate commit, digest, and expected generation — so an idempotent replay re-sends the
identical request. **Field-set pinned.**

bundle_digest.description: The candidate's byte-exact bundle digest (the consent hash) — part of the op's bound identity.

candidate_commit.description: The built commit (`version_id`) this op publishes / reverts / reviews — part of the op's bound identity.

channel.description:
The `--to` channel placement sent alongside a publish/propose so a replay re-sends the identical
value. `None` when no placement was requested (and for a revert/review and pre-existing WALs).

display_name.description:
The skill's advisory display name (the author's folder name) sent alongside a publish/propose so a
replay re-sends the identical value. Advisory only — it names the follower's folder + the dashboard
entry, never the digest or the op's bound identity. `None` for a revert/review and for pre-existing WALs.

expected_generation.description: The `(epoch,seq)` this op's compare-and-set targets — part of the op's bound identity.

good.description:
The GOOD version a `revert` restores (the wire `good`) — present only for a `Revert` op (the
server builds the forward commit from it; it is NOT the `candidate_commit`, so a replay must carry
it). `None` for every other op.

last_receipt.description: The stored terminal receipt, once one is known (the source of idempotent-retry truth).

op.description:
The operation kind (the kind is part of the op's identity — an approve never replays as a
reject).

skill_id.description: The skill this op targets — part of the device-op bound identity.

workspace_id.description: The workspace this op targets — part of the device-op bound identity.

Generation.description:
The internal anti-replay counter `(epoch, seq)`. NEVER rendered to a user; the
agent consumes `expected`/`current` on a CONFLICT.

OpKind.description:
The device operation an [`OpRecord`] carries — the durable record of a replayed write's kind (the op
kind an idempotent retry re-sends; the kind otherwise rides the route). snake_case on the wire/disk.

oneOf.description:
`review --withdraw` — the author retracting their own proposal. A DISTINCT kind from
`ReviewReject` so a crashed reject and a fresh withdraw (or vice versa) can never replay each
other's stored receipt under a reused op id.

Receipt.description:
The ONE canonical receipt across all outcomes — the durable idempotency record (present even
on failures): one stable receipt per op, identical on retry.

created_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp (the plane stamps it; never an ambient clock in `topos-core`).

op_id.description: Client-minted UUIDv4, persisted before the first send.

schema_version.description: Always `1` for this contract version (the schema pins it `const`).

TerminalOutcome.description: The closed set of terminal outcomes the agent branches on. SCREAMING_SNAKE on the wire.

oneOf.description:
A successfully-opened proposal — an explicit `--propose`, or a direct publish/revert the
per-bundle protection DOWNGRADED to one (the receipt's `details.downgraded` says which).
